varieties.name: 1. Australian Teak / Crow's Ash (Flindersia australis) 🏆
varieties.origin: Native to Queensland and New South Wales, Australia (pre-1788)
varieties.traits: Large semi-deciduous tree, 30-40m tall, compound leaves with 5-13 leaflets, scaly brown bark, fast-growing
varieties.yield_notes: Reaches 3-6m in 12 years with good conditions. Can exceed 40m at maturity.
varieties.availability: Widely available at Queensland nurseries including Daley's Fruit Tree Nursery (Pullenvale), Aussie Tree Care (Gold Coast), Sequoia Valley Farms, The Diggers Club

varieties.name: 2. Queensland Silver Ash (Flindersia australis local form) 🏆
varieties.origin: Queensland-specific ecotype, selected for silver-grey bark characteristics
varieties.traits: Distinguished by distinctive silver-grey bark color, compound glossy green leaves, spotted bark appearance from flaking
varieties.availability: Queensland native plant nurseries, local council tree programs

varieties.name: 3. New Guinea Teak Plantations
varieties.origin: Papua New Guinea plantation-grown Tectona grandis
varieties.traits: Golden-brown heartwood with grey/red tinges, straight grain, high natural oil content, 'greasy' feel
varieties.availability: Available as timber through Brisbane suppliers like Narangba Timbers, Paradise Timbers. Seedlings/plants rarely available locally.

varieties.name: 4. Burmese Teak (Myanmar Provenance) 🏆
varieties.origin: Myanmar (Burma) natural forests, considered world's highest quality teak
varieties.traits: Rich golden-brown heartwood, exceptionally high oil content, tight grain, high silica content (from sandy Myanmar soils)
varieties.availability: Available only as imported timber, not as plants in Australia

varieties.name: 5. Indonesian Teak (Plantation) 🧬
varieties.origin: Java, Indonesia - sustainable plantations, FSC-certified sources available
varieties.traits: Light golden to medium brown, tight grain, moderate oil content (lower than Burmese), influenced by volcanic soil minerals
varieties.availability: Available as imported timber through Queensland suppliers

varieties.name: 1. Nilambur Teak (തേക്ക്/Thekku) 🏆
varieties.origin: Nilambur, Malappuram District, Kerala - World's first teak plantation (1842-1846)
varieties.traits: Exceptional size (up to 50m height, 2.5m diameter), rich mahogany-golden brown color, distinctive grain pattern. Tree No. 23 in Conolly's Plot: 4.29m girth, 49.2m length - world's largest plantation teak.
varieties.yield_notes: At Nilambur: 6.93m height, 6.3cm dbh at 5 years; 22.83m height, 45.85cm dbh at 50 years. Significant growth acceleration after 30 years.
varieties.availability: Nilambur Teak Nursery (est. 2004), Kerala Forest Department nurseries, select Kerala private nurseries. Seeds collected from superior mother trees along Chaliyar River.

varieties.name: 2. Kannimara Teak (World's Oldest) 🏆
varieties.origin: Parambikulam Wildlife Sanctuary, Palakkad District, Kerala
varieties.traits: Ancient growth form, massive buttressed trunk, exceptional heartwood quality due to extreme age
varieties.availability: Protected specimen, not available for propagation. Genetic material preserved at Kerala Forest Research Institute.

varieties.name: 3. Travancore Teak 🏆
varieties.origin: Historical Travancore region (Southern Kerala: Thiruvananthapuram, Kollam, Pathanamthitta districts)
varieties.traits: Similar to Nilambur teak but adapted to higher rainfall areas of southern Western Ghats
varieties.availability: Kerala Forest Department seed production areas, Kollam district nurseries

varieties.name: 4. Ranni Teak
varieties.origin: Ranni forest division, Pathanamthitta District (near Thiruvalla)
varieties.traits: Locally recognized as 'second best' teak in Kerala after Nilambur. High-quality timber from natural forests.
varieties.availability: Limited - natural forest provenance. Contact Pathanamthitta Forest Division for authorized seed sources.

varieties.name: 5. Konni Teak 🏆
varieties.origin: Konni, Pathanamthitta District - Kerala's first Reserve Forest (1888)
varieties.traits: Medium-elevation teak adapted to Western Ghats foothill conditions
varieties.availability: Kerala Forest Department - Pathanamthitta Division nurseries

varieties.name: 6. Malayattoor Teak
varieties.origin: Malayattoor Forest Division, Ernakulam District
varieties.availability: Limited - Kerala Forest Department controlled areas

varieties.name: 7. Central Provinces (CP) Teak / Madhya Pradesh Teak 🏆
varieties.origin: Madhya Pradesh (historical Central Provinces), India's largest teak-growing state
varieties.traits: Exceptionally strong and durable, adapted to drier conditions than Kerala teak, lacks golden-brown color of coastal teak
varieties.availability: Seeds from Madhya Pradesh Forest Department seed orchards, available through inter-state forestry programs

varieties.name: 8. Nagpur Teak 🏆
varieties.origin: Nagpur region, Maharashtra, India
varieties.traits: Similar to CP teak - strong, adapted to 1,200-1,800mm rainfall, moderate oil content
varieties.availability: Maharashtra Forest Department seed sources

varieties.name: 9. Bastar Teak 🏆
varieties.origin: Bastar region, Chhattisgarh (formerly Madhya Pradesh)
varieties.traits: Renowned for excellent timber quality, adapted to 1,200-1,500mm rainfall
varieties.availability: Chhattisgarh Forest Department

varieties.name: 10. Ballarshah Teak
varieties.origin: Ballarshah (Chandrapur), Maharashtra
varieties.traits: Good timber quality, moderate growth rate
varieties.availability: Maharashtra Forest Department, National Teak Germplasm Bank

varieties.name: 11. CSO Walayar Clones (20 selections)
varieties.origin: Clonal Seed Orchard II, Walayar, Palakkad District, Kerala (established 1976)
varieties.traits: Selected from moist Western Ghats forests (10°N latitude). Phenotypes chosen for growth and form characteristics.
varieties.availability: Kerala Forest Research Institute (KFRI), Peechi. Seeds from CSO progeny testing at Nilambur.

varieties.name: 12. CSO Topslip Clones (15 selections)
varieties.origin: Clonal Seed Orchard I, Topslip, Tamil Nadu (border with Kerala, established 1976)
varieties.traits: 13 clones common with Walayar CSO. Selected from Western Ghats moist forests.
varieties.availability: Institute of Forest Genetics and Tree Breeding (IFGTB), Coimbatore, Tamil Nadu

varieties.name: 13. Kulathupuzha CSO Clones (31 selections)
varieties.origin: Clonal Seed Orchard, Kulathupuzha, Kollam District, Kerala (established 1985)
varieties.traits: 1,200 trees of 31 clones planted at 8×8m spacing. Genetic diversity studies conducted.
varieties.availability: Kerala Forest Department, Kollam Division

varieties.name: 14. KFRI Superior Tree Selections (41 clones)
varieties.origin: Selected from plantations throughout Kerala (0.3-0.5% selection intensity)
varieties.traits: 30%+ increase in height and girth compared to plantation averages. Clonal multiplication via tissue culture and grafting.
varieties.availability: Kerala Forest Research Institute, clonal multiplication program

varieties.name: 15. TG-5 Clone (IFGTB)
varieties.origin: Institute of Forest Genetics and Tree Breeding (IFGTB), Coimbatore
varieties.traits: Disease-free, pest-resistant, uniform growth, genetically identical plants. Licensed tissue culture production.
varieties.availability: Santhi Online Plants Nursery (Coimbatore), licensed tissue culture labs across Kerala and Tamil Nadu

varieties.name: 16. Burma Teak Tissue Culture Clones
varieties.origin: Selected from Burmese genetic material, propagated via tissue culture
varieties.traits: High oil content genetics from Myanmar provenances, disease-free production, 7-8 year harvest potential with intensive management
varieties.availability: CelGen Biotech, Mother Agri Biotech Laboratories (Bangalore), Sai Shakti Bio Technology (Chhattisgarh), TruTeak (Tamil Nadu)

varieties.name: 17. Nilambur Tissue Culture Selections 🧬
varieties.origin: Elite clones from Nilambur GI-tagged teak, propagated via tissue culture
varieties.traits: Preserves superior Nilambur genetics, rapid clonal multiplication, maintains GI characteristics
varieties.availability: Sai Shakti Bio Technology, specialized Nilambur teak nurseries

varieties.name: 18. CSO Maredumilli Clones (Andhra Pradesh)
varieties.origin: Central State Organization plantations, Maredumilli, Rajahmundry, Andhra Pradesh (raised 1974)
varieties.traits: 30-31 year evaluation: categorized as best, average, and poor performing based on mean annual increment
varieties.availability: Andhra Pradesh Forest Department

varieties.name: 19. CSO Behrai Clones (Madhya Pradesh)
varieties.origin: Teak Seed Orchard, Behrai, Seoni, Madhya Pradesh (123 clones from 8 forest divisions)
varieties.traits: Leaf flushing studies: 18 clones show early leaf flushing (mid-May), 78 show mid flushing (early June), 27 show late flushing (late June)
varieties.availability: Madhya Pradesh Forest Department seed orchards

varieties.name: 20. CSO Angul Clones (Orissa)
varieties.origin: Clonal Seed Orchard, Silvicultural Research Station, Angul, Odisha (25 clones, 30-31 years)
varieties.traits: Latin Square Design with 25 replications, significant inter-clonal variation
varieties.availability: Odisha Forest Department

varieties.name: 21. YSG Biotech TG1-8 Clones
varieties.origin: YSG Biotech commercial clones, tested under various site conditions
varieties.traits: 8 clones evaluated for site adaptability and wood quality
varieties.availability: YSG Biotech and licensed producers

varieties.name: 22. Myanmar (Burmese) Teak - Natural Forest 🏆
varieties.origin: Myanmar natural forests (Tenasserim, Pegu Hills) - world's premium teak source
varieties.traits: Highest natural oil content, tight grain, rich golden-brown heartwood, exceptional silica content from sandy soils
varieties.availability: Limited - tissue culture labs using Myanmar genetics (not direct wild seed)

varieties.name: 23. Thailand Teak 🏆
varieties.origin: Thailand tropical forests, northern Thailand deciduous forests
varieties.traits: Rich golden-brown color, high natural oil content, excellent moisture and pest resistance
varieties.availability: Limited - sustainable Thai plantations produce seed but export restricted. Tissue culture alternatives.

varieties.name: 24. Indonesia Java Teak (Plantation)
varieties.origin: Java, Indonesia - world's largest sustainable teak plantation area (Perum Perhutani state enterprise)
varieties.traits: Light golden to medium brown, tight consistent grain, volcanic soil influences (higher calcium/potassium content)
varieties.availability: Indonesian plantation seed available through forestry trade networks. FSC-certified sources prioritized.

varieties.name: 25. Sri Lanka Teak
varieties.origin: Sri Lanka - introduced 1680, naturalized plantations
varieties.availability: Limited international availability

varieties.name: 26. Bangladesh Teak
varieties.origin: Bangladesh - native range, tropical monsoon forests
varieties.availability: Limited - check with South Asian forestry networks

varieties.name: 27. Laos Teak
varieties.origin: Northwestern Laos - second genetic center of origin (with Myanmar)
varieties.availability: Extremely limited international availability

varieties.name: 28. Tectona hamiltoniana (Dahat Teak)
varieties.origin: Endemic to Myanmar, relatively small native distribution
varieties.traits: Relatively resistant to teak defoliator and skeletonizer insects. Crosses attempted with T. grandis for pest resistance (embryo abortion occurs).
varieties.availability: Not commercially available - conservation breeding programs only

varieties.name: 29. Tectona philippinensis (Philippine Teak)
varieties.origin: Endemic to Philippines, small native distribution
varieties.availability: Not commercially available

varieties.name: 30. T. grandis f. canescens
varieties.growing_notes: Densely canescent (hairy/grayish) on underside of leaves

varieties.name: 32. T. grandis f. punctata
varieties.growing_notes: Hairy only on larger veins on underside of leaf

varieties.name: 33. T. grandis f. tomentella
varieties.growing_notes: Dense yellowish tomentose (woolly) hairs on lower leaf surface

varieties.name: 39. Seed Production Areas (SPA)
varieties.availability: Seeds from Kerala Forest Department designated SPAs

varieties.name: 40. Plus Tree Selections
varieties.availability: Clone banks established by grafting, Kerala Forest Research Institute
